messages.content: What is the princiPal project and what problem does it solve?

princiPal bridges Visual Studio 2022 and VS Code/Cursor debuggers to AI editors using the Model Context Protocol (MCP). Extensions read live debugger state (locals, call stack, breakpoints, source context) and POST it to an ASP.NET Core server on localhost:9229. The server stores rolling breakpoint snapshots and exposes MCP tools so assistants can query runtime state without manual copy-paste.
How are the .NET projects layered in this solution?

PrinciPal.Domain holds value objects and entities (e.g. DebugState, DebugStateStore). PrinciPal.Common has Result types and shared errors. PrinciPal.Application defines abstractions (IDebugQueryService, ISessionManager, ISourceFileReader) and CompactFormatter. PrinciPal.Infrastructure implements SessionManager, DebugQueryService, ThreadSafeDebugStateStore, SourceFileReader. PrinciPal.Server is the host: REST endpoints, MCP tool registration, Quartz idle shutdown. PrinciPal.VsExtension is the VS 2022 VSIX; PrinciPal.VsCodeExtension is TypeScript for VS Code/Cursor using the Debug Adapter Protocol.
Where are MCP tools defined and how do they report errors?

MCP tools live in PrinciPal.Server.Tools.DebugTools, a class marked with [McpServerToolType]. Each method is decorated with [McpServerTool(Name = "...", ReadOnly = true)] and calls IDebugQueryService. The private Resolve method maps Result<string> success to the return value and throws ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpException with the error description on failure, so MCP clients see structured failures from typed errors in the application layer.
List the MCP tool names exposed by DebugTools and what each is for.

list_sessions — connected sessions; get_debug_state — full break-mode state; get_locals — variables with optional depth; get_call_stack — frames; get_source_context — ~30 lines around current line; get_breakpoints — breakpoint list; get_expression_result — last watch/immediate result; explain_current_state — combined source + locals + stack; get_breakpoint_history — snapshot index summary; get_snapshot — one snapshot by index with detail/depth; explain_execution_flow — trace across snapshots with optional deltas.
How does SessionManager resolve the session parameter passed to MCP tools?

ResolveByNameOrId first tries an exact dictionary key match on session ID (case-insensitive). If that fails, it finds entries whose SessionInfo.Name equals the query ignoring case. Zero matches yield SessionNotFoundError; more than one yields AmbiguousSessionError listing candidates. A single name match returns that session's IDebugStateStore.
What happens when GetOrCreateSession is called on the server?

SessionManager uses a ConcurrentDictionary of SessionEntry. For a new sessionId it creates a ThreadSafeDebugStateStore and SessionInfo (SessionId, Name, SolutionPath, ConnectedAt, LastSeen). Empty name or solution path can be filled in on later calls. LastSeen is updated to UtcNow on every call as a heartbeat. The store is returned for the extension to update.
How does DebugStateStore manage breakpoint history?

DebugStateStore keeps current state, optional last ExpressionResult, and a list of DebugStateSnapshot. On Update, if IsInBreakMode is true it appends a snapshot with monotonic Index, CapturedAt UtcNow, and the state. MaxHistorySize defaults to 50; when full, the oldest snapshot is removed from the list. TotalCaptured counts all indices ever assigned, including evicted snapshots, so clients can tell missing indices from never-created ones.
What REST routes does DebugStateEndpoints register?

Under /api/sessions/{sessionId}/debug-state: POST / pushes a full DebugState (GetOrCreateSession then store.Update); POST /expression pushes ExpressionResult; DELETE / clears current state if the session exists; GET /history returns snapshot list; DELETE /history clears history.
What is IdleShutdownJob responsible for?

A Quartz job [DisallowConcurrentExecution] that drives server self-shutdown. It tracks phases: WaitingForFirstSession (shutdown if no session before initial timeout), Active (when session count hits zero move to grace), GracePeriod (shutdown after grace if still zero sessions). It uses ISessionManager.SessionCount and IServerLifecycleManager.RequestShutdown so extensions do not force-kill the process.
What does DebugEventCoordinator do in the VS extension?

It orchestrates IDebuggerReader and IDebugStatePublisher without COM or HTTP types. ShouldPushState returns false when debugging another VS instance. BuildDebugState runs on the UI thread: sets IsInBreakMode, then reads location, locals, call stack, and breakpoints, logging failures per section but still returning partial state. PublishStateAsync, ClearStateAsync, RegisterAsync, and DeregisterAsync delegate to the publisher.
How does DebugQueryService format output for LLMs?

After resolving the session store and validating break mode (and non-null state where needed), it builds strings with CompactFormatter: sections like [loc], [locals], [stack]; variables as name:type=value with nested members indented using dot prefix and max depth; call stack lines like index: FunctionName (File.cs:line). explain_execution_flow and snapshot tools use formatter helpers for diffs and traces depending on detail parameters.
What does CompactFormatter.FormatVariables do at depth 0?

It emits each variable as name:type=value. If maxDepth is 0 and Members is non-empty, it appends [+N] where N is the member count instead of recursing. Invalid values get a [!] suffix. For indent > 0 it prefixes the line with spaces and a leading dot for nested members.
Where are tests located and what stacks do they cover?

tests/unit mirrors projects: PrinciPal.VsExtension.Tests (C# coordinator and HTTP publisher), PrinciPal.Infrastructure.Tests, PrinciPal.VsCodeExtension.Tests (Jest for TypeScript adapters). tests/integration has McpServerIntegrationTests. tests/smoke includes TokenBudgetTests. README notes npm ci && npm test under tests/unit/PrinciPal.VsCodeExtension.Tests for Jest.
Why is ThreadSafeDebugStateStore separate from DebugStateStore?

DebugStateStore is a pure domain model documented as not thread-safe. Infrastructure wraps it in ThreadSafeDebugStateStore for concurrent HTTP/MCP access while preserving the same IDebugStateStore contract used by SessionManager.
When would AmbiguousSessionError occur?

When ResolveByNameOrId is given a session name string that matches more than one active SessionInfo.Name (case-insensitive). The error includes a multi-line description listing each matching name, id, and solution path so the user can disambiguate.
How do Release builds package the server for extensions?

README states that dotnet build runs npm install and tsc for the VS Code extension, and Release build dotnet publish bundles PrinciPal.Server as a self-contained executable into both VSIX outputs. Debug dev uses dotnet run against sibling PrinciPal.Server via a .devproject marker.
How do VS and VS Code extensions share port 9229?
Both target the same localhost server. A lock file coordinates startup so whichever extension starts first launches the server and the other reuses it, avoiding duplicate listeners on the same port.
What adapter interfaces exist in PrinciPal.VsExtension?

IDebuggerReader abstracts reading from the debugger; IDebugStatePublisher abstracts HTTP push and session register/deregister; IExtensionLogger logs messages. VsDebuggerAdapter and HttpDebugStatePublisher are concrete implementations; DebugEventCoordinator depends only on the interfaces for testability.
